name: fallow
description: Codebase analyzer for TypeScript/JavaScript: unused code, circular dependencies, code duplication, complexity hotspots, and architecture boundary violations

global_flags.name: --root
global_flags.description: Project root directory

global_flags.name: --config
global_flags.description: Path to config file (.fallowrc.json, .fallowrc.jsonc, fallow.toml, or .fallow.toml)

global_flags.name: --format
global_flags.description: Output format (alias: --output)

global_flags.name: --quiet
global_flags.description: Suppress progress output

global_flags.name: --no-cache
global_flags.description: Disable incremental caching

global_flags.name: --threads
global_flags.description: Number of parser threads

global_flags.name: --changed-since
global_flags.description: Only report issues in files changed since this git ref (e.g., main, HEAD~5)

global_flags.name: --diff-file
global_flags.description: Unified diff for line-level scoping. Use `-` to read from stdin. Project-level findings still bypass this filter. When both this and `--changed-since` are set, the diff filter wins for finding scope while `--changed-since` still drives file discovery

global_flags.name: --diff-stdin
global_flags.description: Read the unified diff from stdin. Equivalent to `--diff-file -`

global_flags.name: --churn-file
global_flags.description: Import change history from a `fallow-churn/v1` JSON file instead of `git log`, powering hotspots, ownership, and bus-factor on projects with no git repository (Yandex Arc, Mercurial, Perforce). A small wrapper translates your VCS log into the contract. Resolved relative to `--root`. Affects `health --hotspots` / `--ownership` / `--targets` only; `audit`, `impact`, and `--changed-since` still require git

global_flags.name: --max-file-size
global_flags.description: Skip source files larger than this many megabytes (default 5) instead of parsing them, guarding against the out-of-memory blowup a single multi-MB generated/vendored/bundled file causes on large repos. Use `0` for no limit. Declaration files (`.d.ts`) are always analyzed. Skipped files are reported and excluded from every analysis. Also settable via `FALLOW_MAX_FILE_SIZE`

global_flags.name: --baseline
global_flags.description: Compare against a previously saved baseline file

global_flags.name: --parent-run
global_flags.description: Correlate this run with a previous telemetry analysis run

global_flags.name: --save-baseline
global_flags.description: Save the current results as a baseline file

global_flags.name: --production
global_flags.description: Production mode: exclude test/story/dev files, only start/build scripts, report type-only dependencies

global_flags.name: --no-production
global_flags.description: Force production mode OFF for every analysis, overriding a project config's `production: true` (and `FALLOW_PRODUCTION`). Conflicts with `--production`

global_flags.name: --production-dead-code
global_flags.description: Run dead-code analysis in production mode when using bare combined mode

global_flags.name: --production-health
global_flags.description: Run health analysis in production mode when using bare combined mode

global_flags.name: --production-dupes
global_flags.description: Run duplication analysis in production mode when using bare combined mode

global_flags.name: --workspace
global_flags.description: Scope output to selected workspaces. Accepts exact names, glob patterns, and `!`-prefixed negations. Values can be comma-separated or repeated

global_flags.name: --changed-workspaces
global_flags.description: Scope output to workspaces touched since the given git ref. Git is required. Mutually exclusive with `--workspace`

global_flags.name: --group-by
global_flags.description: Group output by owner or by directory

global_flags.name: --performance
global_flags.description: Show pipeline performance timing breakdown

global_flags.name: --explain
global_flags.description: Include metric definitions and rule descriptions in output

global_flags.name: --explain-skipped
global_flags.description: Show a per-pattern breakdown for default duplicate ignores

global_flags.name: --summary
global_flags.description: Show only category counts without individual items

global_flags.name: --ci
global_flags.description: CI mode: equivalent to --format sarif --fail-on-issues --quiet

global_flags.name: --fail-on-issues
global_flags.description: Exit with code 1 if issues are found

global_flags.name: --sarif-file
global_flags.description: Write SARIF output to a file (in addition to the primary --format output)

global_flags.name: --output-file
global_flags.description: Write the report to a file instead of stdout, for any --format (no ANSI codes). Useful on large projects where the terminal scrollback truncates the top. Progress and the confirmation stay on stderr

global_flags.name: --fail-on-regression
global_flags.description: Fail if issue count increased beyond tolerance compared to a regression baseline

global_flags.name: --tolerance
global_flags.description: Allowed issue count increase before a regression is flagged

global_flags.name: --regression-baseline
global_flags.description: Path to the regression baseline file

global_flags.name: --save-regression-baseline
global_flags.description: Save the current issue counts as a regression baseline

global_flags.name: --only
global_flags.description: Run only specific analyses when no subcommand is given

global_flags.name: --skip
global_flags.description: Skip specific analyses when no subcommand is given

global_flags.name: --dupes-mode
global_flags.description: Override duplication detection mode in combined mode

global_flags.name: --dupes-threshold
global_flags.description: Override duplication threshold in combined mode

global_flags.name: --dupes-min-tokens
global_flags.description: Override the minimum token count for clones in combined mode

global_flags.name: --dupes-min-lines
global_flags.description: Override the minimum line count for clones in combined mode

global_flags.name: --dupes-min-occurrences
global_flags.description: Override the minimum clone occurrences in combined mode (must be >= 2)

global_flags.name: --dupes-skip-local
global_flags.description: Only report cross-directory duplicates in combined mode

global_flags.name: --dupes-cross-language
global_flags.description: Enable cross-language duplicate detection in combined mode

global_flags.name: --dupes-ignore-imports
global_flags.description: Exclude module wiring from duplicate detection in combined mode (default). Pass `--dupes-no-ignore-imports` to count it again

global_flags.name: --dupes-no-ignore-imports
global_flags.description: Count module wiring as clone candidates in combined mode (opt out of the default exclusion)

global_flags.name: --score
global_flags.description: Compute health score in combined mode

global_flags.name: --trend
global_flags.description: Compare current health metrics against the most recent saved snapshot

global_flags.name: --save-snapshot
global_flags.description: Save a vital signs snapshot for trend tracking in combined mode. Provide a path or omit for the default `.fallow/snapshots/` location

global_flags.name: --coverage
global_flags.description: Path to Istanbul coverage data for exact CRAP scores in combined mode. Also settable via `FALLOW_COVERAGE` or `health.coverage`

global_flags.name: --coverage-root
global_flags.description: Absolute prefix to strip from Istanbul file paths in combined mode. Also settable via `FALLOW_COVERAGE_ROOT` or `health.coverageRoot`

global_flags.name: --include-entry-exports
global_flags.description: Report unused exports in entry files instead of auto-marking them as used

commands.name: dead-code
commands.description: Analyze project for unused code and circular dependencies

flags.name: --unused-files
flags.description: Only report unused files

flags.name: --unused-exports
flags.description: Only report unused exports

flags.name: --unused-deps
flags.description: Only report unused dependencies

flags.name: --unused-types
flags.description: Only report unused type exports

flags.name: --private-type-leaks
flags.description: Opt in to private type leak API hygiene findings and only report that issue type

flags.name: --unused-enum-members
flags.description: Only report unused enum members

flags.name: --unused-class-members
flags.description: Only report unused class members

flags.name: --unused-store-members
flags.description: Only report unused store members

flags.name: --unprovided-injects
flags.description: Only report unprovided injects

flags.name: --unrendered-components
flags.description: Only report unrendered components

flags.name: --unused-component-props
flags.description: Only report unused component props

flags.name: --unused-component-emits
flags.description: Only report unused component emits

flags.name: --unused-component-inputs
flags.description: Only report unused component inputs

flags.name: --unused-component-outputs
flags.description: Only report unused component outputs

flags.name: --unused-svelte-events
flags.description: Only report unused Svelte dispatched events

flags.name: --unused-server-actions
flags.description: Only report unused server actions

flags.name: --unused-load-data-keys
flags.description: Only report unused SvelteKit load() data keys

flags.name: --unresolved-imports
flags.description: Only report unresolved imports

flags.name: --unlisted-deps
flags.description: Only report unlisted dependencies

flags.name: --duplicate-exports
flags.description: Only report duplicate exports

flags.name: --circular-deps
flags.description: Only report circular dependencies

flags.name: --re-export-cycles
flags.description: Only report re-export cycles

flags.name: --boundary-violations
flags.description: Only report boundary violations

flags.name: --policy-violations
flags.description: Only report rule-pack policy violations

flags.name: --stale-suppressions
flags.description: Only report stale suppressions

flags.name: --unused-catalog-entries
flags.description: Only report unused pnpm catalog entries

flags.name: --empty-catalog-groups
flags.description: Only report empty pnpm catalog groups

flags.name: --unresolved-catalog-references
flags.description: Only report unresolved pnpm catalog references

flags.name: --unused-dependency-overrides
flags.description: Only report unused pnpm dependency overrides

flags.name: --misconfigured-dependency-overrides
flags.description: Only report misconfigured pnpm dependency overrides

flags.name: --include-dupes
flags.description: Also run duplication analysis and cross-reference with dead code

flags.name: --trace
flags.description: Trace why an export is used/unused (format: `FILE:EXPORT_NAME`)

flags.name: --trace-file
flags.description: Trace all edges for a file (imports, exports, importers)

flags.name: --trace-dependency
flags.description: Trace where a dependency is used

flags.name: --impact-closure
flags.description: Compute the impact closure for a file (the transitive affected-but-not-in-diff set + coordination gap). Walks reverse-deps and re-export chains; powers the `inspect_target` MCP tool

flags.name: --top
flags.description: Show only the top N items per category

flags.name: --file
flags.description: Only report issues in the specified file(s). Accepts multiple values. The full project graph is still built, but only issues in matching files are reported. Useful for lint-staged pre-commit hooks

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Watch for changes and re-run analysis

flags.name: --no-clear
flags.description: Don't clear the screen between re-analyses

commands.name: inspect
commands.description: Inspect one file or exported symbol as a bundled evidence query

flags.name: --file
flags.description: File to inspect

flags.name: --symbol
flags.description: Exported symbol to inspect, formatted as FILE:EXPORT

flags.name: --symbol-chain
flags.description: OPT-IN: also attach the best-effort symbol-level call chain (`fallow trace`) as the `symbol_chain` evidence section. Only meaningful for a `--symbol` target. Default off (best-effort, syntactic, OFF the ranked path)

commands.name: trace
commands.description: Trace a symbol's call chain (best-effort, syntactic; OFF the ranked path)

flags.name: symbol
flags.description: Target symbol, formatted as FILE:SYMBOL (e.g. src/utils.ts:formatDate)

flags.name: --callers
flags.description: Walk UP to callers (modules that import the symbol). When neither `--callers` nor `--callees` is set, both directions are walked

flags.name: --callees
flags.description: Walk DOWN to callees (the symbol's module's import-symbol edges plus unresolved call sites). When neither flag is set, both are walked

flags.name: --depth
flags.description: Chain depth bound for both directions (default 2). Symbol-level is best-effort, so a shallow bound keeps the trace legible

commands.name: fix
commands.description: Auto-fix issues: remove unused exports, dependencies, and enum members; add duplicate-export rules to a fallow config file

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Dry run, show what would be changed without modifying files

flags.name: --yes
flags.description: Skip confirmation prompt (required in non-TTY environments like CI or AI agents)

flags.name: --no-create-config
flags.description: Refuse to create a new fallow config file when none exists. Use this from pre-commit hooks, CI bots, and `fallow watch` where silently materialising a new top-level config file would surprise the user. The duplicate-export config-add path is skipped with an explanatory message; source-file edits proceed normally

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize a .fallowrc.json configuration file, AGENTS.md guide, or git pre-commit hook. Use `.fallowrc.jsonc` for editor-native JSON-with-comments support; both extensions are auto-discovered

flags.name: --toml
flags.description: Generate TOML instead of JSONC

flags.name: --agents
flags.description: Scaffold a starter AGENTS.md guidance file for coding agents

flags.name: --hooks
flags.description: Scaffold a shell-level pre-commit git hook in `.git/hooks/` that runs fallow on changed files. Alias for `fallow hooks install --target git`

flags.name: --branch
flags.description: Fallback base branch/ref for the pre-commit hook when no upstream is set

flags.name: --decline
flags.description: Record that this project deliberately stays unconfigured: persists a decline so the first-contact setup hint and the `setup` next-step stop appearing here. Writes no config file; idempotent

commands.name: hooks
commands.description: Install or remove fallow-managed Git and agent hooks

commands.name: ci
commands.description: CI helpers for PR/MR feedback envelopes

commands.name: config-schema
commands.description: Print the JSON Schema for fallow configuration files

commands.name: plugin-schema
commands.description: Print the JSON Schema for external plugin files

commands.name: rule-pack-schema
commands.description: Print the JSON Schema for rule pack files

commands.name: rule-pack
commands.description: Manage declarative rule packs (policy-as-code)

commands.name: guard
commands.description: Show which architecture rules apply to files before changing them

flags.name: files
flags.description: Files to report on (root-relative or absolute; may not exist yet)

commands.name: config
commands.description: Show the resolved config and which config file was loaded

flags.name: --path
flags.description: Print only the config file path (one line, no JSON)

commands.name: list
commands.description: List discovered entry points, files, plugins, boundaries, and workspaces

flags.name: --entry-points
flags.description: Show entry points

flags.name: --files
flags.description: Show all discovered files

flags.name: --plugins
flags.description: Show active plugins

flags.name: --boundaries
flags.description: Show architecture boundary zones, rules, and per-zone file counts

flags.name: --workspaces
flags.description: Show monorepo workspaces and any workspace-discovery diagnostics (malformed package.json, unreachable glob matches, missing tsconfig references)

commands.name: workspaces
commands.description: Show monorepo workspaces and any workspace-discovery diagnostics

commands.name: dupes
commands.description: Find code duplication / clones across the project

flags.name: --mode
flags.description: Detection mode: strict, mild, weak, or semantic (defaults to the value in `.fallowrc.jsonc`, or `mild` if unset)

flags.name: --min-tokens
flags.description: Minimum token count for a clone (defaults to the value in `.fallowrc.jsonc`, or `50` if unset)

flags.name: --min-lines
flags.description: Minimum line count for a clone (defaults to the value in `.fallowrc.jsonc`, or `5` if unset)

flags.name: --min-occurrences
flags.description: Minimum number of occurrences before a clone group is reported. Raise to focus on widespread copy-paste worth refactoring and skip pair-only clones. (defaults to the value in `.fallowrc.jsonc`, or `2` if unset)

flags.name: --threshold
flags.description: Fail if duplication exceeds this percentage (0 = no limit) (defaults to the value in `.fallowrc.jsonc`, or `0` if unset)

flags.name: --skip-local
flags.description: Only report cross-directory duplicates

flags.name: --cross-language
flags.description: Enable cross-language detection (strip TS type annotations for TS↔JS matching)

flags.name: --ignore-imports
flags.description: Exclude module wiring from clone detection (default; covers imports, re-exports, and top-level static require bindings). Pass `--no-ignore-imports` to count it again

flags.name: --no-ignore-imports
flags.description: Count module wiring as clone candidates (opt out of the default exclusion)

flags.name: --top
flags.description: Show only the N most-duplicated clone groups (sorted by instance count descending, then line count descending)

flags.name: --trace
flags.description: Trace all clones at a specific location (format: `FILE:LINE`)

commands.name: health
commands.description: Analyze function complexity (cyclomatic + cognitive)

flags.name: --max-cyclomatic
flags.description: Maximum cyclomatic complexity threshold (overrides config)

flags.name: --max-cognitive
flags.description: Maximum cognitive complexity threshold (overrides config)

flags.name: --max-crap
flags.description: Maximum CRAP score threshold (overrides config, default 30.0). Functions meeting or exceeding this score are reported alongside complexity findings. Pair with `--coverage` for accurate scoring

flags.name: --top
flags.description: Show only the N most complex functions

flags.name: --sort
flags.description: Sort by: cyclomatic (default), cognitive, lines, or severity

flags.name: --complexity
flags.description: Show only complexity findings (functions exceeding thresholds). By default all sections are shown; use this to select only complexity

flags.name: --complexity-breakdown
flags.description: Include the per-decision-point complexity breakdown (`contributions[]`) on each complexity finding in `--format json` output. Each entry names the construct (if, else-if, loop, boolean operator, ...) and its cyclomatic/cognitive weight, so a consumer can explain WHY a function scored high. Used by the VS Code inline editor breakdown. Off by default to keep CI/default output lean

flags.name: --file-scores
flags.description: Show only per-file health scores (fan-in, fan-out, dead code ratio, maintainability index). Requires full analysis pipeline (graph + dead code detection). Sorted by risk-aware triage concern: lower MI and higher CRAP risk first. --sort and --baseline apply to complexity findings only, not file scores

flags.name: --coverage-gaps
flags.description: Show only static test coverage gaps: runtime files and exports with no dependency path from any discovered test root. Requires full analysis pipeline

flags.name: --hotspots
flags.description: Show only hotspots: files that are both complex and frequently changing. Combines git churn history with complexity data. Requires a git repository

flags.name: --ownership
flags.description: Attach ownership signals to hotspot entries: bus factor, contributor count, declared CODEOWNERS owner, and ownership drift. Implies `--hotspots`. Requires a git repository

flags.name: --ownership-emails
flags.description: Privacy mode for author emails emitted with `--ownership`. Defaults to `handle` (local-part only). Use `raw` for OSS repos where authors are public, or `anonymized` to emit non-reversible pseudonyms in regulated environments. Implies `--ownership`

flags.name: --targets
flags.description: Show only refactoring targets: ranked recommendations based on complexity, coupling, churn, and dead code signals. Requires full analysis pipeline

flags.name: --css
flags.description: Add structural CSS analytics: specificity hotspots, !important density, over-complex selectors, deep nesting, and conservative cleanup candidates. Standard CSS is parsed structurally; preprocessor sources are scanned only where fallow can avoid expanding Sass/Less semantics

flags.name: --effort
flags.description: Filter refactoring targets by effort level (low, medium, high). Implies --targets

flags.name: --score
flags.description: Show only the project health score (0–100) with letter grade (A/B/C/D/F). The score is included by default when no section flags are set

flags.name: --min-score
flags.description: Fail if the health score is below this threshold (0-100). Implies --score. The authoritative CI quality gate: when set, complexity findings become informational and the exit code is driven solely by the score (so --min-score 0 always exits 0). Composes with --min-severity (fails if either gate trips). Plain `fallow health` (no gate flag) stays advisory and exits 1 on any finding; for a gate on newly-introduced complexity use `fallow audit --gate new-only`

flags.name: --min-severity
flags.description: Only exit with error for findings at or above this severity. Use --min-severity critical to ignore moderate/high findings in CI. Composes with --min-score (the run fails if either gate trips)

flags.name: --report-only
flags.description: Print the score and findings but never fail CI (always exit 0). Advisory mode for surfacing health in logs without blocking. Mutually exclusive with --min-score and --min-severity

flags.name: --since
flags.description: Git history window for hotspot analysis (default: 6m). Accepts durations (6m, 90d, 1y, 2w) or ISO dates (2025-06-01)

flags.name: --min-commits
flags.description: Minimum number of commits for a file to be included in hotspot ranking (default: 3)

flags.name: --save-snapshot
flags.description: Save a vital signs snapshot for trend tracking. Defaults to `.fallow/snapshots/{timestamp}.json` if no path is given. Forces file-scores, hotspot, and score computation for complete metrics

flags.name: --trend
flags.description: Compare current metrics against the most recent saved snapshot. Reads from `.fallow/snapshots/` and shows per-metric deltas with directional indicators. Implies --score

flags.name: --coverage
flags.description: Path to coverage data (coverage-final.json) for exact per-function CRAP scores. Generate with `jest --coverage`, `vitest run --coverage --provider istanbul`, or any Istanbul-compatible tool. Requires Istanbul format (not v8/c8 native format). Accepts a single Istanbul coverage map JSON file or a directory containing coverage-final.json. Use --coverage-root when the file was generated in a different environment (CI runner, Docker). Affects CRAP scores only, not --coverage-gaps. Also configurable via FALLOW_COVERAGE env var

flags.name: --coverage-root
flags.description: Absolute prefix to strip from file paths in coverage data before prepending the project root. Use when coverage was generated in a different environment (CI runner, Docker). Example: if coverage paths start with /home/runner/work/myapp and the project root is ./, pass --coverage-root /home/runner/work/myapp

flags.name: --runtime-coverage
flags.description: File or directory containing runtime coverage input. Accepts a V8 coverage directory, a single V8 JSON file, or a single Istanbul coverage map JSON file (commonly coverage-final.json)

flags.name: --min-invocations-hot
flags.description: Threshold for hot-path classification

flags.name: --min-observation-volume
flags.description: Minimum total trace volume before the sidecar allows high-confidence `safe_to_delete` / `review_required` verdicts. Below this the sidecar caps confidence at `medium` to protect against overconfident verdicts on new or low-traffic services. Omit to use the sidecar's spec default (5000)

flags.name: --low-traffic-threshold
flags.description: Fraction of total trace count below which an invoked function is classified as `low_traffic` rather than `active`. Expressed as a decimal (e.g. `0.001` for 0.1%). Omit to use the sidecar's spec default (0.001)

commands.name: flags
commands.description: Detect feature flag patterns in the codebase

flags.name: --top
flags.description: Show only the top N flags

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Explain one fallow issue type without running an analysis

flags.name: issue_type
flags.description: Issue type, issue label, or rule id to explain

commands.name: audit
commands.description: Audit changed files for dead code, complexity, duplication, and styling

flags.name: --production-dead-code
flags.description: Run dead-code analysis in production mode for this audit

flags.name: --production-health
flags.description: Run health analysis in production mode for this audit

flags.name: --production-dupes
flags.description: Run duplication analysis in production mode for this audit

flags.name: --dead-code-baseline
flags.description: Compare dead-code issues against a saved baseline (produced by `fallow dead-code --save-baseline`)

flags.name: --health-baseline
flags.description: Compare health findings against a saved baseline (produced by `fallow health --save-baseline`)

flags.name: --dupes-baseline
flags.description: Compare duplication clone groups against a saved baseline (produced by `fallow dupes --save-baseline`)

flags.name: --max-crap
flags.description: Maximum CRAP score threshold (overrides config, default 30.0). Functions meeting or exceeding this score cause audit to fail. Pair with `--coverage` for accurate scoring

flags.name: --coverage
flags.description: Path to Istanbul-format coverage data (coverage-final.json) for accurate per-function CRAP scores in the health sub-analysis. Also configurable via FALLOW_COVERAGE

flags.name: --coverage-root
flags.description: Absolute prefix to strip from coverage data paths before CRAP matching. Use when coverage was generated under a different checkout root in CI or Docker

flags.name: --no-css
flags.description: Disable styling analytics in audit

flags.name: --css-deep
flags.description: Enable deep CSS analysis for audit explicitly: project-wide styling reachability, narrowed back to changed anchors. Deep CSS is on by default; use this to override `audit.cssDeep = false`

flags.name: --no-css-deep
flags.description: Disable deep CSS analysis while keeping local styling analytics on

flags.name: --gate
flags.description: Which findings affect the audit verdict

flags.name: --runtime-coverage
flags.description: Paid runtime-coverage sidecar input. Accepts a V8 directory, a single V8 JSON file, or an Istanbul coverage map JSON. Spawns the `fallow-cov` sidecar as part of the audit pipeline so the `hot-path-touched` verdict surfaces alongside dead-code and complexity findings without requiring a second `fallow health` invocation in CI. License-gated; the verdict is informational (no exit code change) until a future `--gate hot-path-touched` knob lands

flags.name: --min-invocations-hot
flags.description: Threshold for hot-path classification, forwarded to the sidecar when `--runtime-coverage` is set

flags.name: --gate-marker
flags.description: Internal marker identifying a gate run (e.g. `pre-commit`), set by the generated git hook so Fallow Impact can record a containment event when the gate blocks then clears. Hidden; never changes the verdict, exit code, or output

flags.name: --brief
flags.description: Render the deterministic review brief instead of the gating audit report. The brief answers "where do I look?" rather than "will CI block this?", runs the same analysis, and ALWAYS exits 0 (the verdict is carried informationally). Implied by `fallow review`. Orthogonal to `--format`

flags.name: --max-decisions
flags.description: Cap on the number of consequential structural decisions surfaced in the review brief's decision surface (the working-memory limit). Default 4; clamped to the 3-5 band (4 plus or minus 1). Only consulted on the brief path

flags.name: --walkthrough-guide
flags.description: Emit the agent-contract WALKTHROUGH GUIDE: the current digest (brief + decision surface), the review direction, the JSON schema the agent must return, and a deterministic graph-snapshot hash pinned into the digest. The digest is built from the graph only (PR prose is never folded in, so it is injection-resistant). Implies the brief; always exits 0. A thin agent skill calls this to fetch the current guide, produces judgment JSON, then reopens with `--walkthrough-file`

flags.name: --walkthrough-file
flags.description: Ingest an agent's judgment JSON and POST-VALIDATE it against the LIVE graph. Rejects any judgment whose `signal_id` fallow did not emit (anti-hallucination); refuses the whole payload as stale when the echoed graph-snapshot hash no longer matches (the tree moved). The verifier is the graph, not a second model. Implies the brief; always exits 0. The agent's free-text framing is fenced as non-deterministic and never gates or auto-posts

flags.name: --walkthrough
flags.description: Render the existing walkthrough guide as a staged HUMAN terminal tour (Stage 1 load-bearing / Stage 2 mechanical), or markdown with `--format markdown`. Implies the brief; always exits 0. `--format json --walkthrough` emits the same agent-contract JSON as `--walkthrough-guide`

flags.name: --mark-viewed
flags.description: Record one or more changed files as VIEWED in the local walkthrough viewed-state ledger (`.fallow/walkthrough-state.json`), then render the tour. Files already viewed (and still current) collapse into the Cleared panel. Repeatable. Stale marks (the tree moved) are ignored on render but never deleted. Only consulted on the `--walkthrough` path

flags.name: --show-cleared
flags.description: Expand the Cleared panel in the human/markdown walkthrough tour: list each de-prioritized and already-viewed file instead of the collapsed one-line summary. Only consulted on the `--walkthrough` path

flags.name: --show-deprioritized
flags.description: Expand the de-prioritized units in the review brief's weighted focus map ("show me what you de-prioritized"). The `deprioritized` escape-hatch list is ALWAYS present in `--format json` regardless; this flag only re-expands the collapse-by-default human focus render. Only consulted on the brief path

commands.name: decision-surface
commands.description: Surface the consequential structural DECISIONS a change embeds (the apex of the review brief), each framed as a judgment question with the routed expert to ask

flags.name: --max-decisions
flags.description: Cap on the number of surfaced decisions (the working-memory limit). Default 4; clamped to the 3-5 band (4 plus or minus 1)

commands.name: impact
commands.description: Show what fallow has done for you: how many issues it is surfacing, the trend since the last recorded run, and how many commits it contained at the pre-commit gate

flags.name: --all
flags.description: Aggregate every tracked project into one cross-repo roll-up ("what has fallow done for me across all my repos"). Reads the user config dir; ignores `--root`. Cannot combine with a subcommand

flags.name: --sort
flags.description: Row ordering for `--all` (default: most recently recorded first)

flags.name: --limit
flags.description: Cap the number of `--all` rows printed (grand totals still reflect every tracked project)

commands.name: security
commands.description: Surface local security candidates for downstream agent verification (opt-in)

flags.name: --runtime-coverage
flags.description: Paid runtime-coverage sidecar input. Accepts a V8 directory, a single V8 JSON file, or an Istanbul coverage map JSON. When set, `fallow security` annotates tainted-sink candidates with production runtime state and uses that state as an additive ranking signal

flags.name: --min-invocations-hot
flags.description: Threshold for hot-path classification, forwarded to the sidecar when `--runtime-coverage` is set

flags.name: --file
flags.description: Only report security candidates in or reachable from the specified files. The full project graph is still built, but output is scoped to matching finding anchors or trace hops. Accepts multiple values

flags.name: --gate
flags.description: Opt-in regression gate: fail (exit 8) only when the change introduces a NEW security-sink candidate in the changed lines, not on the whole candidate backlog. Requires a diff source: `--changed-since <ref>`, `--diff-file <path>`, or `--diff-stdin`. There is deliberately no `all` mode (gating on the full backlog is the anti-feature this gate avoids)

flags.name: --surface
flags.description: Include the agent-facing attack-surface inventory in JSON output

commands.name: schema
commands.description: Dump fallow's capability manifest (CLI commands and flags, issue types, MCP tools, framework plugins, env vars) as machine-readable JSON for agent introspection. Always JSON, regardless of --format

commands.name: ci-template
commands.description: Print or vendor CI integration templates

commands.name: migrate
commands.description: Migrate configuration from knip, jscpd, or stylelint to fallow

flags.name: --toml
flags.description: Generate `fallow.toml` instead of JSONC

flags.name: --jsonc
flags.description: Write JSONC content to `.fallowrc.jsonc` instead of `.fallowrc.json`. The generated content is the same JSONC (with `//` comments) either way; the `.jsonc` extension lets editors auto-detect JSON-with-comments syntax highlighting and silences linters that flag comments in `.json`. Without `--jsonc` or `--toml`, fallow auto-mirrors the source extension: a `knip.jsonc` migration writes `.fallowrc.jsonc`, a `knip.json` migration writes `.fallowrc.json`

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Only preview the generated config without writing

flags.name: --from
flags.description: Path to source config file (auto-detect if not specified)

commands.name: license
commands.description: Manage the license for continuous/cloud runtime monitoring

commands.name: telemetry
commands.description: Manage opt-in product telemetry

commands.name: coverage
commands.description: Runtime coverage workflow

commands.name: setup-hooks
commands.description: Install or remove a Claude Code PreToolUse hook that gates `git commit` / `git push` on `fallow audit`, so the agent cleans findings before the command runs

flags.name: --agent
flags.description: Target a specific agent surface (default: auto-detect)

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Print what would be written or removed without touching the filesystem

flags.name: --force
flags.description: Overwrite a user-edited hook script, invalid settings.json, or remove a user-edited script during uninstall

flags.name: --user
flags.description: Write to the user's home directory instead of the project root

flags.name: --gitignore-claude
flags.description: Append `.claude/` to the project's `.gitignore`

flags.name: --uninstall
flags.description: Remove the fallow-gate handler, hook script, and AGENTS.md managed block instead of installing them. Idempotent: reports "unchanged" when nothing to remove

default_behavior: Runs all analyses (check + dupes + health). Use --only/--skip to select.

issue_types.description: File is not reachable from any entry point
issue_types.label: Unused Files

issue_types.description: Export is never imported
issue_types.label: Unused Exports

issue_types.description: Type export is never imported
issue_types.label: Unused Types

issue_types.description: Exported signature references a private type
issue_types.label: Private Type Leaks
issue_types.note: Opt-in API hygiene check; the rule defaults to off

issue_types.description: Dependency listed but never imported
issue_types.label: Unused Dependencies
issue_types.note: --unused-deps controls unused-dependency, unused-dev-dependency, unused-optional-dependency, type-only-dependency, and test-only-dependency

issue_types.description: Dev dependency listed but never imported
issue_types.label: Unused Dev Dependencies
issue_types.note: --unused-deps controls unused-dependency, unused-dev-dependency, unused-optional-dependency, type-only-dependency, and test-only-dependency

issue_types.description: Optional dependency listed but never imported
issue_types.label: Unused Optional Dependencies
issue_types.note: --unused-deps controls unused-dependency, unused-dev-dependency, unused-optional-dependency, type-only-dependency, and test-only-dependency

issue_types.description: Production dependency only used via type-only imports
issue_types.label: Type-Only Dependencies
issue_types.note: Only reported in --production mode; --unused-deps scopes it together with the other dependency kinds

issue_types.description: Production dependency only imported by test files
issue_types.label: Test-Only Dependencies
issue_types.note: Not reported in --production mode (test files are excluded there); --unused-deps scopes it together with the other dependency kinds

issue_types.description: devDependency imported by production code with a runtime import
issue_types.label: Dev Dependencies Used in Production

issue_types.description: Enum member is never referenced
issue_types.label: Unused Enum Members

issue_types.description: Class member is never referenced
issue_types.label: Unused Class Members

issue_types.description: Store member is never accessed by any consumer
issue_types.label: Unused Store Members

issue_types.description: Import could not be resolved
issue_types.label: Unresolved Imports

issue_types.description: Dependency used but not in package.json
issue_types.label: Unlisted Dependencies

issue_types.description: Export name appears in multiple modules
issue_types.label: Duplicate Exports
issue_types.note: fallow fix can add an ignoreExports rule to the fallow config instead of editing source

issue_types.description: Circular dependency chain detected
issue_types.label: Circular Dependencies

issue_types.description: Two or more barrel files re-export from each other in a loop
issue_types.label: Re-Export Cycles

issue_types.description: Import crosses a configured architecture boundary
issue_types.label: Boundary Violations
issue_types.note: Requires configured boundary zones (boundaries config)

issue_types.description: Source file matches no configured architecture boundary zone
issue_types.label: Boundary Coverage
issue_types.note: Requires boundaries.coverage.requireAllFiles

issue_types.description: Zoned file calls a callee its zone forbids
issue_types.label: Boundary Call Violations
issue_types.note: Requires boundaries.calls.forbidden patterns

issue_types.description: Banned usage matched a rule-pack rule
issue_types.label: Policy Violations
issue_types.note: Requires a configured rule pack (rulePacks config)

issue_types.description: Suppression comment or tag no longer matches any issue
issue_types.label: Stale Suppressions
issue_types.note: Fix by removing the stale suppression marker itself

issue_types.description: Suppression comment omits a required reason
issue_types.label: Missing Suppression Reasons

issue_types.description: Catalog entry not referenced by any workspace package
issue_types.label: Unused Catalog Entries

issue_types.description: Named catalog group has no entries
issue_types.label: Empty Catalog Groups

issue_types.description: package.json references a catalog that does not declare the package
issue_types.label: Unresolved Catalog References

issue_types.description: pnpm.overrides entry targets a package not declared or resolved
issue_types.label: Unused Dependency Overrides

issue_types.description: pnpm.overrides entry has an unparsable key or value
issue_types.label: Misconfigured Dependency Overrides

issue_types.description: "use client" file exports a server-only / route-config name
issue_types.label: Invalid Client Exports
issue_types.note: Requires the project to declare next

issue_types.description: Barrel re-exports both a "use client" module and a server-only module
issue_types.label: Mixed Client/Server Barrels
issue_types.note: Requires the project to declare next

issue_types.description: "use client" / "use server" directive is not in the leading position and is ignored
issue_types.label: Misplaced Directives
issue_types.note: Requires the project to declare next

issue_types.description: inject() / getContext() reads a key that no provide() / setContext() supplies
issue_types.label: Unprovided Injects

issue_types.description: A Vue / Svelte component is reachable through a barrel but rendered nowhere
issue_types.label: Unrendered Components

issue_types.description: A Vue, Svelte, or React component prop is referenced nowhere in its own component
issue_types.label: Unused Component Props

issue_types.description: A Vue <script setup> defineEmits event is emitted nowhere in its own component
issue_types.label: Unused Component Emits

issue_types.description: An Angular @Input() / signal input() / model() input is read nowhere in its own component
issue_types.label: Unused Component Inputs

issue_types.description: An Angular @Output() / signal output() output is emitted nowhere in its own component
issue_types.label: Unused Component Outputs

issue_types.description: A Svelte component dispatches a createEventDispatcher event whose name is listened to nowhere in the project
issue_types.label: Unused Svelte Events

issue_types.description: A Next.js Server Action exported from a "use server" file is referenced by no code in the project
issue_types.label: Unused Server Actions

issue_types.description: A SvelteKit load() return-object key is read by no consumer
issue_types.label: Unused Load Data Keys

issue_types.description: A React/Preact prop is forwarded unchanged through 3+ pass-through components to a distant consumer
issue_types.label: Prop Drilling
issue_types.note: Opt-in: set rules.prop-drilling to warn or error to enable. Defaults to off.

issue_types.description: A React/Preact component whose whole body is a single spread-forwarded child render (a candidate for inlining)
issue_types.label: Thin Wrappers
issue_types.note: Opt-in: set rules.thin-wrapper to warn or error to enable. Defaults to off.

issue_types.description: Three or more React/Preact components across two or more files declare an identical prop-name set (a missing shared Props type)
issue_types.label: Duplicate Prop Shapes
issue_types.note: Opt-in: set rules.duplicate-prop-shape to warn or error to enable. Defaults to off.

issue_types.description: Two or more Next.js App Router route files resolve to the same URL
issue_types.label: Route Collisions

issue_types.description: Sibling Next.js dynamic route segments use different slug names at the same position
issue_types.label: Dynamic Segment Conflicts

issue_types.description: Function has high cyclomatic complexity
Function has high cognitive complexity
Function exceeds both complexity thresholds
Function has a high CRAP score (complexity combined with low coverage)
File identified as a high-priority refactoring candidate
CSS or CSS-in-JS hardcoded styling value bypasses the design token system
issue_types.label: CSS Token Drift

issue_types.description: CSS or CSS-in-JS declaration block is duplicated across rules
issue_types.label: CSS Duplicate Block

issue_types.description: CSS selector, nesting, or important usage is structurally complex
issue_types.label: CSS Selector Complexity

issue_types.description: CSS or CSS-in-JS surface appears unused
issue_types.label: CSS Dead Surface

issue_types.description: CSS or CSS-in-JS reference resolves to no stylesheet definition
issue_types.label: CSS Broken Reference

issue_types.description: Runtime-reachable file has no test dependency path
Runtime-reachable export has no test dependency path
Statically unused AND never invoked in production with V8 tracking
issue_types.note: Requires --runtime-coverage input (V8 directory, V8 JSON, or Istanbul map)
issue_types.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

issue_types.description: Statically used but never invoked in production
issue_types.note: Requires --runtime-coverage input (V8 directory, V8 JSON, or Istanbul map)
issue_types.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

issue_types.description: Function was invoked below the low-traffic threshold
issue_types.note: Requires --runtime-coverage input (V8 directory, V8 JSON, or Istanbul map)
issue_types.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

issue_types.description: Runtime coverage could not be resolved for this function
issue_types.note: Requires --runtime-coverage input (V8 directory, V8 JSON, or Istanbul map)
issue_types.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

issue_types.description: Runtime coverage finding
issue_types.note: Requires --runtime-coverage input (V8 directory, V8 JSON, or Istanbul map)
issue_types.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

issue_types.description: Changed hot path combines high CRAP and low test coverage
issue_types.note: Produced by fallow coverage analyze
issue_types.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

issue_types.description: Static and runtime evidence indicate code can be deleted
issue_types.note: Produced by fallow coverage analyze
issue_types.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

issue_types.description: Cold reachable uncovered code needs owner review
issue_types.note: Produced by fallow coverage analyze
issue_types.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

issue_types.description: Hot covered code has high CRAP and should be refactored carefully
issue_types.note: Produced by fallow coverage analyze
issue_types.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

issue_types.description: Duplicated code block
issue_types.label: Code Duplication
issue_types.note: Reported by fallow dupes (and bare fallow / fallow audit)

Provider-prefixed or contextual secret literals require verification

suppression_comments.note: Omit [issue-type] to suppress all issue types. Unknown tokens are silently ignored.

exit_codes.1: Error-severity issues found (per rules config, or --fail-on-issues promotes warn→error)
exit_codes.2: Error (invalid config, invalid input, etc.). When --format json is active, errors are emitted as structured JSON on stdout: {"error": true, "message": "...", "exit_code": 2}

environment_variables.FALLOW_FORMAT: Default output format (json/human/sarif/compact/markdown/codeclimate/gitlab-codequality/pr-comment-github/pr-comment-gitlab/review-github/review-gitlab/badge). CLI --format flag overrides this.
environment_variables.FALLOW_QUIET: Set to "1" or "true" to suppress progress output. CLI --quiet flag overrides this.
environment_variables.FALLOW_PRODUCTION: Set to true/false to override production mode for all analyses.
environment_variables.FALLOW_PRODUCTION_DEAD_CODE: Set to true/false to override production mode for dead-code analysis.
environment_variables.FALLOW_PRODUCTION_HEALTH: Set to true/false to override production mode for health analysis.
environment_variables.FALLOW_PRODUCTION_DUPES: Set to true/false to override production mode for duplication analysis.
environment_variables.FALLOW_REVIEW_GUIDANCE: Set to true to append collapsed guidance blocks to review-github/review-gitlab inline comment bodies.
environment_variables.FALLOW_SUMMARY_SCOPE: Summary scope for pr-comment-github/pr-comment-gitlab: all (default) keeps project-level dependency/catalog/override findings outside the diff filter; diff applies the diff filter to them too. Inline review comments are unaffected.
environment_variables.FALLOW_PR_COMMENT_LAYOUT: Sticky PR comment layout: default, compact, gate-only, or details.
environment_variables.FALLOW_CONSOLIDATED_STATUS: When split PR gate check runs are enabled, truthy values add one aggregate Fallow check alongside the per-gate checks.
environment_variables.FALLOW_DIFF_CONTEXT: Line radius around changed diff lines when scoping findings to a diff in the review/PR-comment formats (default 3).
environment_variables.FALLOW_BOT_LOGIN: Bot or token username treated as fallow's own when reconciling existing PR/MR comments in review-github/review-gitlab. Required when posting with a personal access token (the author then carries a human identity).
environment_variables.FALLOW_API_RETRIES: Maximum HTTP attempts for review-comment reconciliation API calls (default 3).
environment_variables.FALLOW_API_RETRY_DELAY: Floor delay in seconds between HTTP retry attempts (default 2); a server-supplied Retry-After overrides it on 429 responses.
environment_variables.FALLOW_CACHE_DIR: Directory for fallow's persistent analysis cache. Relative paths resolve from the project root and override cache.dir.
environment_variables.FALLOW_CACHE_MAX_SIZE: Extraction cache size cap in megabytes (default 256). Wins over the cache.maxSizeMb config field.
environment_variables.FALLOW_EXTENDS_TIMEOUT_SECS: Timeout in seconds for fetching https:// configs referenced via the extends field (default 5).
environment_variables.FALLOW_COVERAGE: Path to Istanbul coverage data (coverage-final.json) for accurate per-function CRAP scores. CLI --coverage flag overrides this.
environment_variables.FALLOW_MAX_FILE_SIZE: Per-file size ceiling in megabytes for source discovery (default 5; 0 = no limit). CLI --max-file-size flag overrides this.
environment_variables.FALLOW_AUDIT_BASE: Pins the fallow audit comparison base ref when no --base/--changed-since is passed (e.g. upstream/main).
environment_variables.FALLOW_AUDIT_CACHE_MAX_AGE_DAYS: GC threshold in days for reusable audit base-snapshot caches (default 30; 0 disables the sweep).
environment_variables.FALLOW_IMPACT_STORE_MAX_AGE_DAYS: GC threshold in days for per-project fallow impact stores; a recorded run reclaims stores older than this (unset/0 keeps every store forever).
environment_variables.FALLOW_ROOT: Project root used by the review-github/review-gitlab renderers to read source for suggestion blocks. Set it alongside --root when rendering review formats outside the bundled CI integrations.
environment_variables.FALLOW_LICENSE: License JWT (full string) for the paid runtime intelligence layer; intended for shared CI runners.
environment_variables.FALLOW_LICENSE_PATH: File path containing the license JWT.
environment_variables.FALLOW_LICENSE_SKEW_TOLERANCE_SECONDS: Clock-skew tolerance applied to the license JWT's iat claim (default 86400).
environment_variables.FALLOW_COV_BIN: Explicit path override for the fallow-cov runtime-coverage sidecar binary.
environment_variables.FALLOW_COV_BINARY_PATH: Secondary explicit path override for the fallow-cov sidecar, checked after FALLOW_COV_BIN (air-gapped installs, distro-packaged sidecars, shared Docker images).
environment_variables.FALLOW_RUNTIME_COVERAGE_SOURCE: Set to cloud to select cloud runtime coverage in fallow coverage analyze without passing --cloud.
environment_variables.FALLOW_REPO: owner/repo fallback for fallow coverage analyze --cloud when --repo is not passed (otherwise parsed from the git origin remote).
environment_variables.FALLOW_API_URL: Base URL override for fallow cloud API calls (license refresh, trial, coverage uploads).
environment_variables.FALLOW_API_KEY: fallow cloud bearer token for coverage upload commands.
environment_variables.FALLOW_CA_BUNDLE: Path to a PEM certificate bundle for fallow cloud and provider HTTP calls (replaces the default WebPKI roots).
environment_variables.FALLOW_UPDATE_CHECK: Set to off/0/false to disable the human-TTY upgrade nudge and its background version check.
environment_variables.FALLOW_SUGGESTIONS: Set to off/0/false/no/disabled to suppress the next_steps[] array of read-only follow-up commands in JSON output (and the human Next: line). Useful for CI consumers that snapshot-diff raw --format json output. Default on.
environment_variables.FALLOW_TELEMETRY: Opt-in telemetry mode: off, on, or inspect (print the payload to stderr without sending). Telemetry is off by default.
environment_variables.FALLOW_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: Admin/fleet kill switch: truthy values hard-disable telemetry and refuse fallow telemetry enable.
environment_variables.FALLOW_AGENT_SOURCE: Normalized agent vendor for telemetry classification (e.g. claude_code, codex, cursor). Only read when telemetry is on.
environment_variables.DO_NOT_TRACK: Honored as a top-precedence telemetry kill switch (consoledonottrack.com convention).
environment_variables.FALLOW_BIN: Path to the fallow binary (used by the fallow-mcp server to spawn the CLI).
environment_variables.FALLOW_TIMEOUT_SECS: MCP server: per-tool-call CLI subprocess timeout in seconds (default 120). Raise for long runs like production coverage on large dumps.
environment_variables.FALLOW_DIFF_FILE: MCP server: path to a unified diff that scopes all findings by changed line.
environment_variables.FALLOW_CHANGED_SINCE: MCP server: git ref that scopes file discovery for analysis tools.
environment_variables.FALLOW_INTEGRATION_SURFACE: Telemetry integration_surface override for non-CLI surfaces (mcp/lsp/vscode/napi/programmatic). Set by the MCP server on the CLI it spawns.
environment_variables.FALLOW_MCP_TOOL: Telemetry mcp_tool dimension, validated against the MCP tool-name allowlist. Set by the MCP server alongside FALLOW_INTEGRATION_SURFACE=mcp.

mcp_tools.note: key_params is a curated subset; the live MCP input schemas (list_tools) are authoritative for the full parameter list

tools.name: code_execute
tools.description: Run a bounded read-only JavaScript snippet that composes fallow's analysis tools inside a sandbox (Code Mode meta-tool, not a plain analysis call)

tools.name: analyze
tools.description: Full dead-code analysis: unused files, exports, types, dependencies, circular dependencies, and boundary violations

tools.name: check_changed
tools.description: Incremental dead-code analysis scoped to files changed since a git ref (ideal for PR review)

tools.name: security_candidates
tools.description: Unverified local security candidates (tainted sinks) for downstream agent verification

tools.name: inspect_target
tools.description: One evidence bundle for a file or exported symbol: trace, dead-code actions, duplication, complexity, and security candidates

tools.name: guard
tools.description: Report the architecture rules that apply to given files before editing them: boundary zone, allowed import zones, forbidden calls, and rule-pack policies

tools.name: find_dupes
tools.description: Code duplication detection with clone groups and refactoring suggestions

tools.name: check_health
tools.description: Complexity, styling health, hotspots, ownership, refactoring targets, coverage gaps, and CSS/CSS-in-JS candidates

tools.name: check_runtime_coverage
tools.description: Merge V8 or Istanbul runtime coverage into the health report (hot paths, cold paths, verdicts)
tools.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

tools.name: get_hot_paths
tools.description: Production hot paths from runtime coverage, sorted by invocation volume
tools.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

tools.name: get_blast_radius
tools.description: Blast-radius context (caller counts, risk bands) from runtime coverage; augments review only, never gates safe_to_delete (three-state tracking issues that verdict)
tools.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

tools.name: get_importance
tools.description: Production-importance scores (0-100) combining invocations, complexity, and ownership; augments review only, never gates safe_to_delete (three-state tracking issues that verdict)
tools.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

tools.name: get_cleanup_candidates
tools.description: Cleanup candidates with safe_to_delete, review_required, and low_traffic verdicts from runtime coverage
tools.license_note: A single local runtime-coverage capture is free; continuous or multi-capture runtime monitoring requires an active license (fallow license activate).

tools.name: get_token_blast_radius
tools.description: Design-token blast radius for Tailwind v4 @theme tokens AND CSS-in-JS defineVars/createTheme-family token definitions: per token, a consumer_count (static lower bound) and a capped located consumers[] sample tagged theme-var/css-var/utility/apply (Tailwind) or js-member (CSS-in-JS cross-module member access); descriptive context for sizing a token change, never a deletion gate

tools.name: audit
tools.description: Combined dead-code, complexity, duplication, and styling audit for changed files with a pass/warn/fail verdict

tools.name: decision_surface
tools.description: Surface the few consequential structural decisions a change embeds (coupling, public API, dependency), each as a judgment question with the routed expert; ranked, capped, and signal_id-anchored

tools.name: fallow_explain
tools.description: Explain one issue type (rationale, examples, fix guidance) without running an analysis

tools.name: fix_preview
tools.description: Dry-run auto-fix preview; shows what would change without modifying files

tools.name: fix_apply
tools.description: Apply auto-fixes: removes unused exports, dependencies, and enum members (mutates files)

tools.name: project_info
tools.description: Project metadata: active framework plugins, discovered files, entry points, and boundary zones

tools.name: list_boundaries
tools.description: List architecture boundary zones and access rules

tools.name: feature_flags
tools.description: Detect feature flag patterns (environment variables, SDK calls, config objects)

tools.name: impact
tools.description: Read the local Fallow Impact value-tracking report (per-project history in the user config dir, never in the repo; local-dev only)

tools.name: impact_all
tools.description: Roll every tracked fallow project on this machine into one cross-repo value report (hashed keys plus basename labels, never paths; local-dev only)

tools.name: trace_export
tools.description: Trace why an export is used or unused, including re-export chains and entry-point status

tools.name: trace_file
tools.description: Trace all module-graph edges for a file (imports, exports, importers, re-exports)

tools.name: trace_dependency
tools.description: Trace where a dependency is imported and whether scripts or CI use it

tools.name: trace_clone
tools.description: Deep-dive a duplicate-code clone group by location or fingerprint

plugins.note: Built-in framework plugins, auto-activated when their enabler dependency is present; run fallow list --plugins for the set active in a specific project

task_matrix.task: check which architecture rules apply to a file before changing it
